title: Sebestena sebestena
taxonomy: Sebestena sebestena is classified as a member of the family Anacardiaceae, commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. This family includes various species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Sebestena sebestena is a deciduous tree that is native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia. The species is further classified as a genus within the family Anacardiaceae, along with other related genera such as Toxicodendron and Schinus. Sebestena sebestena is a unique and lesser-known species within this family.
uses: Sebestena sebestena, a tropical plant native to Central and South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits.
